name: turbo
context: Turborepo monorepo build system — task pipelines, workspace management, and intelligent caching
info: Turborepo is a high-performance build system for JavaScript and TypeScript monorepos. It orchestrates task execution across workspaces using a dependency-aware task graph, caches outputs locally and remotely to eliminate redundant work, and parallelizes builds across available CPU cores. Agents use this ecosystem to configure turbo.json pipelines, manage workspace structure (apps/ and packages/), enable remote caching for CI, and troubleshoot cache misses. Turborepo works with npm, yarn, pnpm, and bun. Configuration lives in turbo.json at the repo root with optional per-workspace overrides via the extends field. Key sub-skills: [[turbo/config]] for turbo.json setup, [[turbo/pipelines]] for task dependency graphs, [[turbo/workspaces]] for monorepo structure, [[turbo/caching]] for cache behavior, and [[turbo/troubleshooting]] for debugging.
body:
# Turborepo

Turborepo is the standard build system for JavaScript/TypeScript monorepos. It takes your existing package.json scripts and turbo.json configuration, builds a dependency graph across workspaces, and executes tasks in the optimal order with maximum parallelism.

## Core Concepts

- **Tasks** — Scripts defined in package.json, orchestrated by [[turbo/pipelines]] in turbo.json
- **Workspaces** — Individual packages in your monorepo, managed via [[turbo/workspaces]]
- **Caching** — Local and remote caching of task outputs, explained in [[turbo/caching]]
- **Configuration** — turbo.json at repo root, detailed in [[turbo/config]]

## Quick Start

```bash
# Create a new turborepo
npx create-turbo@latest my-monorepo

# Or add to existing monorepo
npm install turbo --save-dev
```

## Minimal turbo.json

```json
{
  "tasks": {
    "build": {
      "dependsOn": ["^build"],
      "outputs": ["dist/**"]
    },
    "dev": {
      "persistent": true,
      "cache": false
    },
    "lint": {},
    "test": {
      "dependsOn": ["build"]
    }
  }
}
```

## CLI Overview

```bash
# Run tasks (turbo is an alias for turbo run)
turbo build                    # Run build in all workspaces
turbo build --filter=web       # Run build only in 'web' workspace
turbo build lint test          # Run multiple tasks
turbo build --affected         # Only workspaces changed on this branch

# Utilities
turbo prune --scope=web        # Create minimal monorepo subset
turbo gen                      # Generate new workspaces from templates
turbo login && turbo link      # Enable remote caching via Vercel

# Debugging
turbo build --dry              # Preview without executing
turbo build --graph=graph.svg  # Visualize task graph
turbo build --summarize        # Generate run summary JSON
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `TURBO_TOKEN` | Authentication token for remote cache |
| `TURBO_TEAM` | Team name/ID for remote cache |
| `TURBO_REMOTE_CACHE_SIGNATURE_KEY` | Signing key for cache artifact integrity |
| `TURBO_SCM_BASE` | Git base ref for --affected (default: main) |
| `TURBO_SCM_HEAD` | Git head ref for --affected (default: HEAD) |
| `TURBO_FORCE` | Force re-execution ignoring cache |
| `TURBO_LOG_ORDER` | Log ordering: stream, grouped, auto |

## Package Manager Support

Turborepo works with all major package managers:
- **npm** — workspaces in package.json
- **yarn** (v1 and berry) — workspaces in package.json
- **pnpm** — workspaces in pnpm-workspace.yaml
- **bun** — workspaces in package.json

sections.title: What is Turborepo
sections.body: Turborepo is a high-performance build system for JavaScript and TypeScript monorepos by Vercel. It analyzes your workspace dependency graph and runs tasks in the optimal order, caching outputs so repeated builds are near-instant. It requires minimal configuration — just a turbo.json file alongside your existing package.json scripts.

sections.title: Installing Turborepo
sections.body:
Install turbo as a devDependency in your monorepo root:

```bash
npm install turbo --save-dev
# or
pnpm add turbo --save-dev --workspace-root
# or
yarn add turbo --dev
```

Alternatively, scaffold a new project with `npx create-turbo@latest`. Add turbo.json to your repo root and define tasks matching your package.json scripts.

sections.title: CLI commands
sections.body:
Primary commands:
- `turbo run <tasks>` (or just `turbo <tasks>`) — execute tasks across workspaces
- `turbo prune --scope=<pkg>` — create a sparse monorepo with only the specified package and its dependencies
- `turbo gen` — generate new workspaces from templates
- `turbo login` / `turbo link` — authenticate and connect to remote cache
- `turbo daemon status` — check the turbo daemon

Key flags: `--filter`, `--affected`, `--dry`, `--force`, `--graph`, `--summarize`, `--concurrency`, `--output-logs`, `--env-mode`

sections.title: Package manager compatibility
sections.body: Turborepo reads workspace configuration from your package manager. npm, yarn, and bun use the `workspaces` field in root package.json. pnpm uses pnpm-workspace.yaml. The `packageManager` field in root package.json is recommended for consistent behavior. Turborepo validates the package manager version at runtime unless `dangerouslyDisablePackageManagerCheck` is set.

links.context: Configure turbo.json — global options, env modes, remote cache settings
Define task dependency graphs with dependsOn, outputs, and caching rules
Structure monorepo workspaces — apps/, packages/, internal packages
Understand local and remote caching, cache inputs/outputs, invalidation
Debug cache misses, slow builds, and common Turborepo issues

related.note: TypeScript projects are the primary use case for Turborepo monorepos
Test tasks are commonly orchestrated via Turborepo pipelines
Lint tasks are a standard part of turbo pipelines
Vite build outputs are cached by Turborepo
Next.js is the most common framework in Turborepo monorepos

author: dojo-community
